title: 10 best herbal essential oils for bronchitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for bronchitis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of this respiratory condition by reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and fighting off infections. The best herbal essential oils for bronchitis include Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Echinacea purpurea, Hyptis suaveolens, Cymbopogon citratus, Mentha x piperita, Melaleuca alternifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Ocimum basilicum. If left untreated, bronchitis can lead to prolonged coughing, wheezing, and difficulty breathing, significantly impacting daily life, causing fatigue, and reducing productivity, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that exacerbate the condition. The oil's expectorant qualities also make it an effective natural remedy for loosening and clearing mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate the discomfort and swelling associated with bronchitis, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.

Antiviral and Antibacterial Properties: The essential oil's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, reducing inflammation and preventing further complications.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving congestion and coughs associated with bronchitis.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus leaves at 100°c for 4-6 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids.
Mix 10 drops of the herbal eucalyptus globulus essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm inflamed airways. The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria, reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and chest tightness. By using thyme essential oils through steam inhalation or massage, individuals can effectively alleviate bronchitis symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Thyme essential oil has been shown to be effective against various bacterial and viral strains that cause respiratory infections, including bronchitis, helping to combat infection and reduce symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The essential oil's ability to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs can help alleviate congestion, coughing, and shortness of breath commonly associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe respiratory tract irritations and ease congestion associated with bronchitis.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in camphor, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and remove mucus from the airways, providing relief from bronchial congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried thyme leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their oils and essences.
Steam distill the crushed thyme mixture with 30 ml of water in a glass apparatus at a temperature of 100°c for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with bronchitis because it contains natural compounds that can help ease congestion, reduce inflammation, and relax airway spasms. The menthol in peppermint oil has a decongestant effect, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe irritated bronchial tubes and reduce coughing. By inhaling peppermint essential oils through steam inhalation or diffusion, individuals with bronchitis may experience relief from symptoms and improved respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Peppermint essential oil helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion, which is especially helpful for people with chronic bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling in the airways and ease inflammation caused by bronchitis, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: Peppermint essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds that help ease congestion and soothe respiratory issues.
Leaves: Contains menthone, which helps thin mucus and ease breathing, making it effective against bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a well-tended mentha x piperita plant for optimal potency and aroma.
Chop the harvested peppermint leaves into small pieces to release their natural oils and essences quickly.
Steam distill the chopped peppermint leaves in a 5:1 ratio with water at 100°c for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil by condensing the steam distillation vapors using a cooled collection vessel within 2 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, rosemary's expectorant properties can loosen mucus, allowing for a productive cough and relieving congestion. The antimicrobial properties of rosemary also help combat infections that may contribute to bronchitis, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, easing congestion and discomfort associated with bronchitis.
Expectorant: The essential oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve respiratory congestion.
Antioxidant: Rosemary essential oil contains powerful antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with bronchitis.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues due to their ability to ease breathing, reduce coughs, and open up airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Steam distill the leaves using a clevenger apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Combine distilled oil with carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond at a 1:5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 24 hours before straining and decanting.
Store the finished essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and borneol, work to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus. Additionally, curry plant oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out congestion from the lungs, providing relief from coughing and wheezing associated with bronchitis. This natural remedy can be used topically or through aromatherapy to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal curry plant essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-caryophyllene, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and chest tightness.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oil contains antimicrobial compounds that can help combat bacterial infections associated with bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae, by inhibiting their growth and replication.

Expectorant properties: Herbal curry plant essential oil has natural expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax airways and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for relieving bronchial congestion.
Seeds: Curry plant seeds contain a high amount of citral, a natural expectorant that helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum flowers in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or air-drying for 3 weeks.
Crush 100g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steam-distill 200ml of water and 1 tablespoon of powdered helichrysum italicum in a still for 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated airways and reduce congestion. The oil's natural compounds, such as isobutylamides, work to calm coughs and open up the airways, making it easier to breathe. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Combine one part dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves with four parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a still at a temperature of 100-140°f for 30-60 minutes.
Collect the essential oils that condense in the condenser coils and separate from the distilled water.
Filter the collected essential oils through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves of cymbopogon citratus ensuring no signs of pests or diseases.
Wash the harvested leaves with warm water to remove dirt and debris taking approximately 10 minutes.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus for about 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water through decantation and filtration procedures.
Store the resulting cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bay rum essential oils helps with bronchitis because it contains antispasmodic and expectorant properties that help to alleviate congestion and coughing. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta racemosa leaves at dawn when their potency is highest and store them in airtight containers until use.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a copper alembic still and a boiling water bath for 4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass bottle and separate it from any impurities by decantation and filtering through paper coffee filters.
Mix the filtered essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 and store it in dark glass containers for up to 6 months.
Perform quality control tests on the final product by analyzing its color appearance and aroma using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry methods within 24 hours of production.

remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum oil content.
Dry freshly harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 12 hours or until crumbly.
Steam distill dried leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with water and essential oil collection device.
Collect 10% artemisia absinthium essential oil by volume after 2-3 hours of distillation processing time.
Filter collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and debris for final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with bronchitis because its antispasmodic properties can relax constricted airways, reducing coughing fits and making it easier to breathe. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom, ensuring at least 1 kilogram of material.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and a small amount of soap to remove dirt and debris, taking about 30 minutes.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a still or a distillation apparatus, processing the material for approximately 4 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Combine the obtained essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5, stirring gently and allowing the mixture to infuse for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles, ready for use or further dilution, taking note of the final yield.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bronchitis with herbal essential oils is to combine eucalyptus oil, known for its decongestant properties, with peppermint oil, which helps relieve coughing. Apply a blend of the two oils topically on the chest and back using a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Inhale the vapor by adding a few drops to a humidifier or diffuser to loosen mucus and ease congestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bronchitis that are treated with herbal essential oils are chronic coughs, asthma, and respiratory congestion. Eucalyptus oil, in particular, is often used to relieve symptoms of these conditions due to its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. Other herbs like peppermint, thyme, and tea tree oil may also be used to soothe the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and opening up airways for easier breathing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its decongestant and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving congestion and coughs associated with the condition. The oil's menthol content helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the oil's camphor content helps to break down phlegm, further easing respiratory symptoms and promoting a sense of relief and comfort. Regular use may help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with bronchitis.
Eucalyptol: Exhibits expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus and other respiratory secretions from the lungs, thereby relieving congestion associated with bronchitis.

Pinene: Demonstrates antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce the severity of bronchitis symptoms by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Eucalyptus globulus due to their high content of eucalyptol, a primary compound responsible for its decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: The barks are used for their high concentration of eucalyptol, which helps to relieve bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are also used to produce essential oils, which contain compounds that help to ease coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote expectoration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants during the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c with 3 parts water to 1 part leaves for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The antimicrobial properties of the oil also help to combat underlying infections that can exacerbate bronchitis, providing relief from congestion and coughing. remedy_constituents: Cineole: This monoterpene acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Camphene: As a monoterpene, camphene helps to reduce inflammation and open up airways, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms such as coughing and difficulty breathing.
Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, alleviating bronchitis symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Stems: Used for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that may accompany bronchitis.
Flowers: Utilized for their antispasmodic properties, which help relax airway muscles and alleviate bronchitis-related coughing and wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and have reached their peak fragrance.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days to prevent mold.
Steam distill 500 grams of the dried leaves with 2 liters of water for 3 to 4 hours to extract the oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel within 2 hours of distillation.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to combat respiratory infections by reducing the production of mucus and alleviating congestion. Additionally, thyme oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out phlegm, making it easier to breathe. Its natural decongestant properties also help to ease coughs and soothe the airways, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms. Carvacrol: Assists with bronchitis by its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and remove mucus from the airways, facilitating easier breathing.
borneol: Aids with bronchitis by its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a primary source of thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of linalool, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and cough associated with bronchitis.

Buds: The buds of Thymus vulgaris contain a rich mixture of compounds, including thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris flowers and leaves by cutting the stems with sharp scissors during the peak bloom season.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris material at a temperature range of 100 to 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and sediment.

Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make essential oils for bronchitis because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers from the field in early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers at 40c in a single layer for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 1l of water at 100c and 1 bar pressure for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and store it in a dark glass bottle with a dropper.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter and label it with the date and echinacea purpurea content.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are a secondary source of the essential oil, which contains similar compounds to the leaves, providing additional benefits in treating bronchitis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest essential oil content approximately 100g.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 15%.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a steam distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity and quality.

Menthone: It has expectorant properties, which help to thin and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with bronchitis.
Limonene: It possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can cause bronchitis, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make essential oils for bronchitis due to their high menthol content, which helps to ease congestion and coughs.
Stems: Used to make essential oils for bronchitis because of their menthone content, which possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe bronchial passages.
Buds: Used to make essential oils for bronchitis due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which can help to relax airways and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of fresh leaves and stems of the plant during peak growth in warm sunny areas.
Steam distill the harvested material in a still at 100-150 mmhg for 4-6 hours.
Collect the distilled water and plant oil mixture and allow it to separate into distinct layers.
Separate the plant oil layer from the water layer by decantation or centrifugation for 2 hours.
Filter the plant oil layer through a charcoal filter to remove impurities and improve quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with bronchitis because it has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in fighting off bacterial and viral infections in the lungs. The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and breathe. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract, providing quick relief from bronchitis symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. This leads to faster recovery and improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Linalool: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate bronchitis.
Eucalyptol: Has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs and airways, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, which is widely used for its antimicrobial and decongestant properties to help relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves also contain compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to ease bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: The essential oil extracted from the leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia is often used in aromatherapy to help open airways and reduce congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency in late summer early fall.
Steam distill the plant material in a steam distiller at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser of the distiller in a glass container and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool dry place to preserve its quality.
Analyze the essential oil for its chemical composition and purity using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry to ensure its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways. This allows for easier breathing and helps to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the oil's soothing and calming effects may also help to reduce coughing and promote relaxation, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid constituent helps to reduce inflammation and protect the respiratory system from damage caused by bronchitis.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin constituent has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to alleviate coughing and loosen mucus in the lungs.

Isoflavans: These flavonoid constituents possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for bronchitis treatment.
Flowers: The flowers contain triterpenes and flavonoids, which have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to loosen mucus and reduce bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 mature roots of glycyrrhiza glabra with a diameter of 2-3 cm for maximum essential oil yield.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum essential oils helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eugenol and linalool, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways and combat bacterial infections that can cause bronchitis. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. This natural remedy can provide relief from bronchitis symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce inflammation and relieve cough associated with bronchitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Eugenol: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by bronchitis, while also reducing inflammation.
Borneol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs, making breathing easier for individuals with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used for essential oil production due to their high concentration of volatile compounds that help relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Ocimum basilicum contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing bronchitis inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are used to produce essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bronchitis-causing pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature ocimum basilicum leaves in the early morning or late evening when the essential oil content is highest.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage of the essential oil.
Steam distill the ocimum basilicum leaves at a temperature of 40-60 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel or a centrifuge.
Store the extracted ocimum basilicum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.
